jokes.text: Release the hounds
jokes.setup: Romantic love song spelling out L-O-V-E
jokes.punchline: Burns' signature threatening catchphrase inserted inappropriately
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only Burns would interrupt a love song this way. Classic Simpsons juxtaposition of romantic and threatening.

jokes.text: For the next two hours, we'll be kid-free. It'll be just like the time we lost them at the mall.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about alone time with Marge at carnival
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to accidentally losing their children
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - treating child endangerment as a fond memory. Dark but perfectly in character.

jokes.text: That was the best Christmas ever.
jokes.setup: Homer's comment about losing the kids at the mall
jokes.punchline: Marge agreeing it was their best Christmas
jokes.explanation: Escalates the darkness - even Marge enjoyed being kid-free. Subverts her usual maternal image.

jokes.text: Nothing gets a woman in your arms faster than scary robots and simulated privacy.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining the Tunnel of Love
jokes.punchline: Reducing romance to calculated mechanical manipulation
jokes.explanation: Homer's crude but not entirely wrong analysis of carnival romance attractions.

jokes.text: if Dad kisses Mom too much, a stork will lay an angel egg in her tummy, and the next thing you know, we find a baby brother in the cabbage patch!
jokes.punchline: Hilariously confused mash-up of different euphemisms
jokes.explanation: Clever combination of multiple 'birds and bees' euphemisms into one nonsensical explanation.

jokes.text: Do whatever you want. I'm getting a candy apple.
jokes.punchline: Bart completely giving up and walking away
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart response - overwhelmed by information, defaults to simple pleasure. Economy of reaction.

jokes.text: You will be charged for the extra time.
jokes.setup: Romantic ride broken down, passengers stuck
jokes.punchline: Still charging them despite the malfunction
jokes.explanation: Standard corporate greed joke - functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Brangelina?
jokes.punchline: Marge suggesting contemporary celebrity couple
jokes.explanation: Dated pop culture reference without additional twist - cheap laugh that ages poorly.

jokes.text: They were the Bonnie and Clyde of their day. Their names were Bonnie and Clyde.
jokes.setup: Homer building up mysterious outlaw couple
jokes.punchline: Revealing they literally are Bonnie and Clyde
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer anticlimax - builds suspense only to state the obvious. Perfect character voice.

jokes.text: This is Texas, future home of Akeem Olajuwon!
jokes.punchline: Reference to future basketball star
jokes.explanation: Anachronistic sports reference - mildly clever but dated and not essential to character.

jokes.text: No one calls me 'chicken' without goading me into doing something stupid!
jokes.setup: Bonnie challenging Clyde to prove he's not chicken
jokes.punchline: Clyde acknowledging he's being manipulated but doing it anyway
jokes.explanation: Self-aware stupidity - character knows he's being manipulated but can't resist. Classic Homer trait.

jokes.text: You could've had anything you want for free! You're a co-owner!
jokes.punchline: Store owner reveals Clyde already owns half the business
jokes.explanation: Solid ironic reversal - the 'crime' is actually just poor business awareness. Well-constructed surprise.

jokes.text: Oats! Texas caviar!
jokes.punchline: Calling plain oats 'Texas caviar'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay elevating mundane grain to luxury status. Fits character's simple pleasures.

jokes.text: Well, there might be others, but I'm too lazy to figure out what they are. So violence it is!
jokes.setup: Clyde thinking violence is Bonnie's only turn-on
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's too lazy to discover alternatives
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - taking the path of least resistance even in romance. Brutally honest self-awareness.

jokes.text: It's a sex thing! Say no more!
jokes.setup: Customer asking why they're being shot at after cooperating
jokes.punchline: Assuming it's sexual and immediately backing off
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection - character assumes sexual motivation for violence and politely withdraws.

jokes.text: Well, deposit yourselves in a seat because you've earned my interest!
jokes.setup: Driver offering ride to rob banks
jokes.punchline: Banking puns about deposits and interest
jokes.explanation: Forced banking wordplay - tries too hard and feels unnatural to the situation.

jokes.text: A nation, crippled by unemployment and bored by Seabiscuit
jokes.punchline: Treating Seabiscuit as boring entertainment
jokes.explanation: Mild historical anachronism joke - dismisses famous racehorse as dull. Weakly executed.

jokes.text: Because gee, it's swell to have all that money.
jokes.setup: Radio show asking why Bonnie filed money under 'G'
jokes.punchline: Wholesome explanation for filing system
jokes.explanation: Marge's wholesome logic applied to criminal organization. Fits character's sweet nature.

jokes.text: I am recalling all our air defense forces from Pearl Harbor. Somebody please remind me to move them back.
jokes.setup: FDR addressing Bonnie and Clyde threat
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly setting up Pearl Harbor vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Dark historical irony - FDR inadvertently creating conditions for Pearl Harbor attack. Clever historical knowledge.

jokes.text: I've been played like a pair of spoons!
jokes.punchline: Folksy metaphor for being manipulated
jokes.explanation: Colorful rural metaphor - fits the character's folksy background well.

jokes.text: Hey, keep it down, will ya? The part where all my men get killed is coming up.
jokes.setup: Sheriff being interrupted while watching a movie
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions his men's deaths as entertainment
jokes.explanation: Dark deadpan humor - treating a tragic outcome as casual entertainment

jokes.text: Unmarried?! That's it. I'm turning them in.
jokes.punchline: Only caring about unmarried cohabitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character priorities - ignores murder and robbery but draws line at moral impropriety.

jokes.text: Even I'm offended by this, and I'm a fat Southern sheriff.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of own likely prejudices
jokes.explanation: Self-aware stereotype acknowledgment - character recognizes his own likely biases while condemning worse.

jokes.text: the battery seems to be dead. I'll get some help by hiding in that bush.
jokes.setup: Sheriff's car won't start during pursuit
jokes.punchline: Plans to get help by hiding, which makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Absurd logic that fits the bumbling sheriff character archetype

jokes.text: Come on, shoot! Shoot! Oh! Not me!
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's become the target
jokes.explanation: Standard mistaken identity gag - competent execution but predictable setup.

jokes.text: Say, Officer, I like how you handle that gun. Thanks. I like the way you take a bullet.
jokes.punchline: Officer complimenting her shooting skills
jokes.explanation: Dark flirtation joke - mutual appreciation of violence as romantic gesture.

jokes.text: Cool. Now we're shooting the birds that are pecking at the bodies.
jokes.punchline: Bart's casual appreciation of escalating violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart reaction - fascinated by violence escalation with childlike detachment.

jokes.text: This is a story of two dogs with a love so strong, no hose could separate them.
jokes.setup: Promise of romantic dog story for children
jokes.punchline: Reference to dogs getting stuck during mating
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre - sounds romantic but references biological reality of dog mating.

jokes.text: A mutt like you could never get a fancy dame like that. Even her fleas have more class than you.
jokes.punchline: Even the parasites are classier
jokes.explanation: Standard class humor with escalation - extends the insult to include parasites for extra degradation.

jokes.text: Oh, no! Chompy and I have taken a cottage in West Leg. It's where all the fleas are fleeing.
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'fleeing' and flea migration
jokes.explanation: Decent pun on 'fleeing/flea' with geographic migration concept. Moderately clever wordplay.

jokes.text: You know, heaven must have left its doggie door open because you are one...
jokes.punchline: Gets interrupted by walking into glass door
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy interrupting romantic moment. Good setup/payoff structure.

jokes.text: I like those chicks with them head cones when they start talking, you can drop below the horizon.
jokes.setup: Asking Lady to bring a friend
jokes.punchline: Preference for dogs with medical cones to avoid conversation
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate humor - Homer's shallow dating preferences translated to dog behavior.

jokes.text: You say it's goin' kinda-a rough? That is precisely what I'm saying.
jokes.setup: Luigi's exaggerated Italian accent asking about the date
jokes.punchline: Homer responding with overly formal diction
jokes.explanation: Mild contrast between Luigi's casual Italian dialect and Homer's suddenly formal response.

jokes.text: You're pretty feisty for an upper-class bitch.
jokes.punchline: Using technical term for female dog as compliment
jokes.explanation: Clever use of technical dog terminology in romantic context - sounds crude but is literally accurate.

jokes.text: Oh, I like that you use the technical term for a female dog.
jokes.punchline: Marge appreciating the technical precision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - appreciating Homer's accidental correctness. Classic character dynamic.

jokes.text: Why didn't I listen to Mama? 'Serve-a food to humans!'
jokes.setup: Health inspector arriving at dog restaurant
jokes.punchline: Luigi's mother's practical business advice
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate regret - Luigi's mother gave sensible business advice he ignored.

jokes.text: And I am in heat. That's the safe time, right? Oh, the safest.
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of fertility
jokes.explanation: Perfect character ignorance - both completely misunderstand dog reproduction, mirroring human contraception confusion.

jokes.text: Is that a foxhunt I hear?
jokes.setup: Lady feeling sick in the morning
jokes.punchline: Homer using hunting sounds as excuse to leave
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer avoidance behavior - using transparent excuse to escape responsibility.

jokes.text: Any minute now You'll be stuck with her brood
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'brood' as offspring and brooding
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay in musical context - 'brood' works both as offspring and emotional state.

jokes.text: Come on, with all this high-class tail around?
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'tail' for dogs vs. sexual slang
jokes.explanation: Double entendre using dog anatomy and sexual slang - works in context but feels slightly forced.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's gotta teach me how to lick my... Oh, I figured it out.
jokes.setup: Puppy wanting father to teach important skill
jokes.punchline: Figures out dog grooming mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good timing gag - suggests inappropriate lesson before revealing innocent dog behavior.

jokes.text: where you're put to sleep... by my boring stories. And then you'll be killed!
jokes.setup: Threatening to put dogs to sleep
jokes.punchline: Clarifying it's literally boredom, then actual death
jokes.explanation: Effective misdirection - plays with euphemism before revealing actual threat. Well-structured surprise.

jokes.text: Unless you want to.
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly offering it as real option
jokes.explanation: Mild cringe humor - character awkwardly doubles down on inappropriate joke.

jokes.text: Gawrsh, you can't kill me. I'm half-human! Look, I wear clothes! I speak English! I'm speaking it right now!
jokes.punchline: Claiming human status with increasingly desperate evidence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and character reference - Goofy's distinctive speech pattern while making logical argument.

jokes.text: It's Cycle 5, specially formulated for dogs with five minutes to live.
jokes.punchline: Dog food marketed for execution timing
jokes.explanation: Dark but original concept - corporate marketing applied to death row scenarios.

jokes.text: This place is no picnic, but it sure beats working for Disney.
jokes.punchline: Reference to Disney employment conditions
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about animation industry - implies Disney working conditions worse than death row.

jokes.text: I want to stay, sit, roll over and beg! Please, please, please, please, please, please, please...!
jokes.setup: Shady asking to join the family
jokes.punchline: Lists dog tricks as if they're emotions, then literally begs
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mixing human emotions with dog commands

jokes.text: Uh... you know, there are nine other puppies.
jokes.punchline: Revealing much larger family size
jokes.explanation: Good escalation reveal - family burden much larger than anticipated. Classic Homer understatement.

jokes.text: Smash the flag Burn the queen Kick the sky Antichrist!
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical escalation ending with 'kick the sky'
jokes.explanation: Solid punk rock parody - escalates from political to impossible ('kick the sky').

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm really lucky you saved me from meeting other people and having new experiences.
jokes.setup: Boyfriend claiming coincidence of following her
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic acknowledgment of controlling behavior
jokes.explanation: Sharp sarcasm - character recognizes and calls out possessive behavior directly.

jokes.text: Hitler and her majesty / Sittin' in a tree / B-O-R-R-I-N-G!
jokes.punchline: Uses children's playground chant format for political rebellion
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of childish format with adult political content

jokes.text: You sicken me!
jokes.punchline: Sid insulting his own fan
jokes.explanation: Perfect punk attitude - rejecting praise maintains authentically hostile persona. Great economy.

jokes.text: I've spelled 'unrequited.' Now I know what it means.
jokes.punchline: Learning vocabulary through personal experience
jokes.explanation: Clever character moment - academic achiever learning through emotion what she previously knew intellectually.

jokes.text: S-A-T-U-R-D-A-Y Night!
jokes.punchline: Spells out Saturday Night like a spelling bee rather than singing it
jokes.explanation: Mild musical parody showing Nancy's influence on Sid's performance style

jokes.text: Look, an American nerd bird. Nerd bird? Would a nerd bird have chocolate, huh? Would a nerd bird? Stop saying 'nerd bird!'
jokes.punchline: Nancy getting increasingly frustrated with repeated insult
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - repeated insult becomes increasingly annoying, chocolate used as defense mechanism.

jokes.text: We're gonna go out and play our songs like we've never played them before: competently!
jokes.punchline: Setting bar incredibly low - just competence
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion - motivational speech about achieving basic competence rather than greatness.

jokes.text: Sidney Sheldon Vicious!
jokes.punchline: Full name includes romance novelist Sidney Sheldon
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition - punk rocker's middle name references romance novelist, undermining tough image.

jokes.text: When I hired a guy named Sid Vicious, I assumed it would be a 30-year business relationship.
jokes.punchline: Unrealistic expectations for punk rock career longevity
jokes.explanation: Good irony - corporate mindset applied to notoriously short-lived punk careers.

jokes.text: Education's bollocks! Bollocks! America is bollocks! Bollocks! Politeness is bollocks! Bollocks! Bollocks!
jokes.punchline: Reducing everything to single repeated expletive
jokes.explanation: Decent punk parody - reduces complex social criticism to repetitive profanity.

jokes.text: Can't reach me bass. Me back is front ward!
jokes.setup: Sid trying to perform on stage
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy of being turned around
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with dialect - Bart's confusion rendered in cockney accent.

jokes.text: Dang it, I wanted to hear what else was bollocks!
jokes.punchline: Audience member genuinely disappointed to miss more repetitive content
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the repetitive performance showing audience engagement

jokes.text: You gobbed up our gig, you git!
jokes.setup: Band angry at Sid for ruining performance
jokes.punchline: British slang for spitting/'gobbing' used as verb
jokes.explanation: British slang usage - 'gobbed up' works as both spat and messed up. Decent wordplay.

jokes.text: Ho!! Just stop talking and bang your head against the wall!
jokes.punchline: Reducing punk music to literal head-banging
jokes.explanation: Literal interpretation of punk aesthetic - head-banging becomes actual instruction.

jokes.text: This song doesn't make me feel angry at all. If anything, it makes my anger ebb!
jokes.setup: Nancy's influence making Sid's music less aggressive
jokes.punchline: Punk fan complaining that music reduces rather than increases anger
jokes.explanation: Good ironic observation about punk music's purpose being subverted by love

jokes.text: 'Cause love is the elixir The potion of emotion The world-saving fixer The lotion of devotion.
jokes.setup: Nancy writing love songs for Sid
jokes.punchline: Overly sweet lyrics completely opposite to punk ethos
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Marge's wholesome nature corrupting punk music with sweetness.

jokes.text: Your music violates everything punk stands for, which is nothing!
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy criticizing Sid's mellowed music
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges punk stands for nothing while still being offended
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradoxical criticism - violating nothing while standing for nothing. Perfect Comic Book Guy pedantry.

jokes.text: Comic Book Guy's Bar
jokes.setup: Reference to famous CBGB's punk venue
jokes.punchline: Replaced with Comic Book Guy from Simpsons
jokes.explanation: Clever character integration - Comic Book Guy as punk venue owner maintains the initials while being completely opposite.
